title: Homogeneity
summary: Defines slot weights, total weight, output weight, and multihomogeneity for multilinear operators.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines slot weights, total weight, output weight, and multihomogeneity for multilinear operators.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows PolyGJMSoperators/Homogeneity.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Pointwise homogeneity for rank-r input tuples. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, w of type fin r maps to the real numbers, and f of type cal conf.multi input r, its defining expression is for every i : Fin r, Cal Conf.Is XHomogeneous w i F i.
correspondence.leanType: def IsMultiHomogeneous {r : ℕ} (w : Fin r → ℝ) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Total input weight. In explicit terms, for calconf of type calculus conf, r in the natural numbers, and w of type fin r maps to the real numbers, its defining expression is ∑ i, w i.
correspondence.leanType: def totalWeight (_CalConf : Calculus Conf) {r : ℕ} (w : Fin r → ℝ) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Expected output weight after lowering total degree by 2N. In explicit terms, for calconf of type calculus conf, r in the natural numbers, w of type fin r maps to the real numbers, and n in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Cal Conf.total Weight w minus 2 times N.
correspondence.leanType: def outputWeight (CalConf : Calculus Conf) {r : ℕ} (w : Fin r → ℝ) (N : ℕ) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r in the natural numbers, w of type Fin r maps to the real numbers, F of type Cal Conf.Multi Input r, hF of type Cal Conf.Is Multi Homogeneous w F, and i of type Fin r, Cal Conf.Is XHomogeneous w i F i.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r\colon \mathbb{N},\;w\colon \operatorname{Fin} r \longrightarrow \mathbb{R},\;F\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiInput} r,\;hF\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsMultiHomogeneous} w F,\;i\colon \operatorname{Fin} r,\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsXHomogeneous} (w i) (F i)
correspondence.leanType: theorem IsMultiHomogeneous.apply {r : ℕ} {w : Fin r → ℝ} {F : CalConf.MultiInput r} (hF : CalConf.IsMultiHomogeneous w F) (i : Fin r) : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (w i) (F i)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For w of type Fin 2 maps to the real numbers, Cal Conf.total Weight w equals w 0 plus w 1.
correspondence.leanType: theorem totalWeight_fin_two (w : Fin 2 → ℝ) : CalConf.totalWeight w = w 0 + w 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For w of type Fin 3 maps to the real numbers, Cal Conf.total Weight w equals w 0 plus w 1 plus w 2.
correspondence.leanType: theorem totalWeight_fin_three (w : Fin 3 → ℝ) : CalConf.totalWeight w = w 0 + w 1 + w 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r in the natural numbers, w of type Fin r maps to the real numbers, and N in the natural numbers, Cal Conf.output weight w N equals Cal Conf.total Weight w minus 2 times N.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeight_def {r : ℕ} (w : Fin r → ℝ) (N : ℕ) : CalConf.outputWeight w N = CalConf.totalWeight w - 2 * (N : ℝ)

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
